reasoning: 1,3-bis(2-hydroxyethoxy)benzene is a benzene ring with two oxyethanol substituents attached via ether linkages (phenyl-O-CH2CH2-OH), making it a phenol ether (aryl alkyl ether). The benzene core places it in benzenoids, and the ether oxygens linking the benzene to the alkyl chains classify it as a phenol ether.
